tool_args.label: New York City Transit Key Performance Metrics - September 2023

payload.title: NEW YORK CITY TRANSIT KEY PERFORMANCE METRICS
payload.description: Key performance metrics document prepared for the September 2023 meeting of the New York City Transit & Bus Committee.
payload.prepared_for: New York City Transit & Bus Committee

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: New York City Transit and MTA Bus operate all subways and buses in New York City. 45,000 employees serve 4,500,000 passengers a day. Operates nearly 6,700 subway cars and 4,500 buses, maintains 472 subway stations, 640 miles of track, 20 bus depots and 70 shops and yards.

North America's largest transportation network, serving a population of 15.3 million people across a 5,000-square-mile travel area surrounding New York City through Long Island, southeastern New York State, and Connecticut. Governed by a 23-member Board of Directors.

payload.entity_name: New York City Transit & Bus Committee

payload.description: Members: Haeda Mihaltses (Chair), Andrew Albert, Jamey Barbas, Randolph Glucksman, David Jones, Frankie Miranda, John-Ross 'JR' Rizzo, John Samuelson, Sherif Soliman, Lisa Sorin, Midori Valdivia

warnings: entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.
entity.organization is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Organization names should link to entity records when supported. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

payload.description: Program to revamp subway stations. 50th subway station revamped in September 2023. Atlantic Ave L was the 48th station to receive upgrades on September 12. On track to revamp over 50 subway stations by end of 2023.

NYC DOT completed five miles of bus lanes along Northern Boulevard in Queens. DOT's longest bus lane installation this year, stretching across Central Queens. Improves Q66, QM2, QM3, QM20 and QM32 bus routes. Connects to M and R trains at Broadway and Northern Boulevard, benefiting over 17,000 daily customers.

Main focus to curb subway crime and violence. Includes surging officers into the transit system to boost uniformed officer presence on platforms and trains, renew efforts to aid people experiencing serious mental health issues, and continuing rollout of cameras in the transit system.
Launched in August with 1,200 Phase 1 customers transitioned over; beginning onboarding over 2,400 new customers in September.

Expanded to over 1000 buses (57 routes). Started testing new automated securement devices on M7 buses allowing customers using wheelchairs to quickly and independently secure their wheelchairs with the push of a button.

payload.project_name: Subway Surfing Kills – Ride Inside, Stay Alive
payload.description: Campaign launched in partnership with the Mayor and Governor to restore customers' trust in the Transit system.

warnings: project.partners is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Partner agency lists are relation context. Suggested relation: has_partner.

payload.description: New barrier system that fully separates the operator compartment from the passenger compartment to help prevent assaults. Implements larger glazing on the compartment door and a fixed glaze in front of the door. One bus retrofitted with the design, a second bus in final stages.

Camera Monitor Systems (CMS) installed on exterior mirrors provide wider view on both sides of the vehicle for easy detection of road hazards and enhanced visibility in inclement weather. Installed on five in-service buses.

Testing new accessibility features at 66 St – Lincoln Center (1), the M66, and other nearby subway stations including NaviLens language translation, wayfinding app, accessible boarding area floor decals, and tactile Braille line maps.
payload.location_text: 66 St – Lincoln Center (1), the M66, and other nearby subway stations

normalized_location.raw_text: 66 St – Lincoln Center (1), the M66, and other nearby subway stations

tool_args.label: Water Main Break at Times Square-42 St

payload.event_name: Water main break at West 41 St & 7 Av
payload.description: Water main break at West 41 St & 7 Av sent water into the station at Times Square-42 St. Hundreds of NYCT workers responded.

First BusTalk events held at three bus stops in Staten Island, Brooklyn and Manhattan. NYCT President Rich Davey and leadership team engaged with bus customers responding to questions, concerns and suggestions related to neighborhood bus service.

NYCT leadership team held a town hall at the 207th Street Overhaul and Maintenance Yard in Inwood. Over 800 employees attended. President Davey, SVP Subways Demetrius Crichlow, SVP Buses Frank Annicaro, and VP Paratransit Chris Pangilinan shared survey findings and answered questions.
Bus Rodeo at Orchard Beach returning after a 6-year hiatus.

tool_args.label: Cops Cameras Care partnership with NYPD

payload.description: 555 buses equipped with ABLE cameras on 19 routes across all five boroughs. Routes showed average speed improvements of over 5% and 20% reductions in collisions. Over 50% of ABLE routes run in equity priority areas impacting 450,000 average daily customers. Deploying to 20th route at end of the month.
